subject_tmpl: Who do I contact about the pothole on {STREET}?
body_tmpl: Hello, I moved to Bellwood about three months ago and I'm still figuring out who handles what. There's a large pothole at {STREET} near {CROSS} that I've almost hit twice now. I wasn't sure if I should call the Village or IDOT. Can someone point me in the right direction? My name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I can be reached at {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, thanks for reaching out. Potholes on {STREET} fall under Village Public Works — we've logged your report and a crew will inspect within {DAYS} business days. For state routes like Mannheim Rd, you'd contact IDOT directly. Feel free to call us at (708) 547-3500 with questions.

subject_tmpl: Question about my water bill — account {ACCT}
body_tmpl: I just received my quarterly bill and the amount is {AMOUNT}, which is about double what I normally pay. I haven't had any leaks that I know of. My account number is {ACCT} and my address is {ADDRESS}. Could you look into this? I'd rather not pay the extra charges if there's been a meter reading error.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for contacting us about account {ACCT}. We've pulled your usage history and will have a billing supervisor review the meter reading. Someone will follow up by {DATE}. If you'd like, you can also schedule a meter re-read by calling (708) 547-3500 ext. 220.

subject_tmpl: New resident — do I need a permit to build a deck?
body_tmpl: Hi there. We recently purchased our home at {ADDRESS} and would like to add a deck off the back. I've heard mixed things — some neighbors say you need a permit, others say it depends on the size. Could you let me know what Bellwood requires? We're planning something roughly {NUM} square feet. Thanks, {FIRST}.

subject_tmpl: Ongoing noise from {ADDRESS} — please help
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I've called the non-emergency line {NUM} times in the past month about loud music coming from {ADDRESS} late at night — usually after {TIME}. Officers have responded but the problem keeps returning. I'm a senior resident and I'm losing sleep. I'm writing to you directly because I don't know where else to turn. Please look into this. — {FIRST} {LAST}, {STREET}.
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, thank you for writing and I'm sorry to hear this has been ongoing. I'm asking Officer Banks to flag this address for enhanced patrol attention and will have our code office check for any related violations. You should hear from my office by {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: Missed garbage pickup on {STREET} — {DATE}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here at {ADDRESS}. Our garbage was not picked up on {DATE}, which is our regular collection day. The cans are still out at the curb. This is the second time in two months. Could someone arrange a make-up pickup or let me know if there was a schedule change I missed?
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, we apologize for the missed collection on {DATE}. A make-up pickup has been scheduled for {STREET} on {DATE}. Please have your cans out by 7 a.m. that morning. We'll look into why the route was missed.

subject_tmpl: Request to meet the Mayor at Taste of Bellwood
body_tmpl: Hello, I'm a longtime Bellwood resident and I'd love to have a few minutes with Mayor Okonkwo at the Taste of Bellwood this year to share some ideas about the {STREET} neighborhood. Is there a way to schedule a quick meet-and-greet, or does the Mayor have walk-around time during the event? My name is {NAME} and my number is {PHONE}.

subject_tmpl: Abandoned vehicle parked on {STREET} for weeks
body_tmpl: There's been an old car — no plates, flat tires — sitting in front of {ADDRESS} on {STREET} since at least {DATE}. Nobody seems to be using it and it's becoming an eyesore. Who handles abandoned vehicle complaints in Bellwood? I wasn't sure if this was Police or Code Enforcement. Thank you, {FIRST}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, abandoned vehicles are handled jointly by Code Enforcement and the Police Department. We've created case {CASE} and notified the PD. If the vehicle has no valid registration it will be tagged for tow within {DAYS} days. We'll keep you posted.

subject_tmpl: Standing water in my yard after every rain — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Dear Village Engineer, after any significant rainfall I get about {NUM} inches of standing water in my backyard that doesn't drain for two or three days. My property is at {ADDRESS} near {CROSS}. A neighbor mentioned there might be a drainage district that covers this area. Can you tell me who I should contact and whether the Village can help? — {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: FOIA request — Village Board minutes from {DATE}
body_tmpl: Good morning. I would like to submit a FOIA request for the approved meeting minutes from the Village Board meeting held on {DATE}. I'm particularly interested in any discussion of the {STREET} streetscape project. Please let me know the process and any applicable fees. My name is {FIRST} {LAST}, email is on file.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for your FOIA request. Under Illinois law we have 5 business days to respond. Your request has been logged as {CASE}. The {DATE} Board minutes are available at no charge and will be emailed to you by {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: Thank you for the {STREET} repaving
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo — I just want to say thank you. The repaving of {STREET} between {CROSS} and {CROSS} has made a huge difference. I've lived on this block for {AGE} years and those cracks were terrible. The crew was professional and even cleaned up afterward. Please pass along our appreciation to Public Works. — {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, what a wonderful note to receive — thank you! I'll absolutely share your kind words with Frank and the Public Works team. It's residents like you who make Bellwood the community it is. Warm regards.

subject_tmpl: How do I set up water service at my new address?
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm moving into {ADDRESS} on {DATE} and need to get water service set up in my name. I've never done this before — is there a deposit required? Can I do everything online or do I have to come in person? Any information you can share would be really helpful. My name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I can be reached at {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Welcome to Bellwood, {FIRST}! To start service, please complete the New Account form on our website or visit Village Hall at 3200 Washington Blvd. A {AMOUNT} deposit is required for new accounts. Bring a photo ID and your lease or closing documents. Service is typically activated within {DAYS} business days.

subject_tmpl: Street light out on {STREET} near {CROSS}
body_tmpl: The street light at {STREET} and {CROSS} has been out for at least {DAYS} nights. It's very dark at that corner and I'm concerned about safety, especially for kids walking to the Metra station in the morning. Is this the Village or ComEd? Just want to make sure it gets to the right department.

subject_tmpl: Status of permit application {CASE}
body_tmpl: Hi Rosa, I submitted a permit application for a bathroom remodel at {ADDRESS} about {DAYS} days ago (case number {CASE}). I haven't heard anything back and my contractor is asking when we can start. Can you give me an update on where things stand? Thank you, {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, your permit {CASE} is currently in plan review. We expect to complete review by {DATE}. If approved with no corrections, you'll receive the permit by email the same day. Please let your contractor know we'll also need a copy of their license on file before the first inspection.

subject_tmpl: Rat sighting near dumpster at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: I wanted to report that I've seen rats — two or three of them — near the dumpster behind {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. This started about two weeks ago. I'm worried they'll spread to my property. Who handles rodent complaints in the Village? Do I call the Health Department or Code Enforcement?
reply_tmpl: Thank you for the report. Rodent complaints are handled by our Health Office. I'm opening an inspection case for {ADDRESS} and will schedule a site visit within {DAYS} business days. Please keep your own bins tightly sealed in the meantime. Case number {CASE} has been assigned.

subject_tmpl: Business license renewal question — {BIZ}
body_tmpl: This is {NAME} from {BIZ} at {ADDRESS}. Our annual business license renewal is coming up and I have a question about the new zoning compliance form that was added this year. Do we need to fill that out even if our use hasn't changed? We've been at this location for {AGE} years. Please advise so we can get our renewal in on time.
reply_tmpl: Hi {NAME}, thanks for asking ahead of the deadline. Yes, the zoning compliance form is required for all renewals this cycle regardless of use change — it's a one-page self-certification. You can find it on our website under Business Licensing. Your renewal is due by {DATE}. Let me know if you have questions.

subject_tmpl: Bulk item pickup — how does it work in Bellwood?
body_tmpl: I'm a newer resident and I have an old couch and a broken dresser to get rid of. Do I need to schedule a bulk pickup or is there a certain day I can put large items out? I haven't found clear information on the Village website. My address is {ADDRESS}. Thank you, {FIRST}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}! Bellwood offers bulk item pickup on the {DATE} of each month. Simply place items at the curb by 7 a.m. on your scheduled day — no pre-registration needed. For items like mattresses, a {AMOUNT} sticker (available at Village Hall) must be affixed. More details at bellwoodil.gov.

subject_tmpl: Suggestion: add more shade at the Taste of Bellwood
body_tmpl: Hi, I attended the Taste of Bellwood last year with my kids and we had a great time, but the heat was brutal in the afternoon — very little shade in the main vendor area. Would the Village consider renting some large canopy tents for next year's event? I think it would make a big difference for families and seniors. Just a thought. — {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Tall grass and weeds at {ADDRESS} — ongoing issue
body_tmpl: The property at {ADDRESS} on {STREET} has had grass over a foot tall for at least {DAYS} days. The owners don't seem to be maintaining it at all. I've reported this twice before (cases {CASE} and {CASE}). Is there a process to escalate when a property is a repeat violator? My yard looks great — my neighbor's doesn't. This is frustrating. — {FIRST}
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, I can see your prior reports in our system. Since {ADDRESS} has now received two notices without compliance, we're escalating to a formal hearing notice. The village may also arrange mowing and bill the property owner. I'll update you once the hearing date is set.

subject_tmpl: Suggestion for the Village — community garden on {STREET}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I've been a Bellwood resident for {AGE} years and I'd love to see a small community garden on the vacant lot at {STREET} and {CROSS}. Other towns in the area have done this and it really brings neighbors together. I'd be happy to help organize volunteers. Who should I talk to about this idea?
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, I love this idea! Please reach out to Kevin O'Brien in our Parks & Events office — he's been exploring exactly this kind of community green space initiative. I'll give him a heads-up that you'll be in touch. Thank you for caring about Bellwood.

subject_tmpl: Signage permit question for {BIZ} at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Good afternoon. We're planning to install a new exterior sign for {BIZ} at {ADDRESS}. It will be a {NUM}-square-foot illuminated wall sign. Do we need a separate electrical permit in addition to the sign permit, and is there a village-specific color restriction on St. Charles Rd? We'd like to get the paperwork in as soon as possible.

subject_tmpl: Sidewalk damage near {ADDRESS} — tripping hazard
body_tmpl: There's a raised sidewalk panel in front of {ADDRESS} on {STREET} that is a real tripping hazard — the edge is about {NUM} inches high. I actually watched an elderly woman stumble on it last week. Is this the property owner's responsibility or does the Village repair sidewalks? Please advise. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, thank you for flagging this safety concern. Sidewalk repairs in Bellwood are a shared responsibility — we'll send an inspector out to assess the panel at {ADDRESS}. If it qualifies for our repair program, we'll handle it at no cost. Expect a response within {DAYS} business days.

subject_tmpl: Referral from PD — property condition at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Sandra, patrol responded to a call at {ADDRESS} on {DATE} and observed significant exterior property issues — boarded windows, debris in the yard, possible illegal dumping. Not a criminal matter but definitely worth a code inspection. Forwarding so your office can open a case. Let me know if you need the incident report. — Reggie

subject_tmpl: Reporting a water main break on {STREET}
body_tmpl: There is water bubbling up from the pavement on {STREET} near {CROSS} right now — looks like a main break. I'm not sure if this is an emergency line or if I email. Please send someone as soon as possible. This is {FIRST} at {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, we've dispatched a Public Works crew to {STREET} and {CROSS}. For water emergencies after hours, please always call (708) 547-3500 for the fastest response. We appreciate you letting us know quickly.

subject_tmpl: FOIA — budget documents FY{DATE} and vendor contracts
body_tmpl: This is a Freedom of Information Act request under 5 ILCS 140. I am requesting: (1) the adopted Village budget for fiscal year {DATE}; (2) all contracts exceeding {AMOUNT} executed with vendors between {DATE} and {DATE}. Please provide in electronic format if available. My name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I am a journalist with {BIZ}.
reply_tmpl: Your FOIA request has been received and logged as {CASE}. Under Illinois law, we will respond within 5 business days. Please note that certain contract exhibits may require legal review before release. We will contact you at this address if an extension or fee estimate is required.

subject_tmpl: Can I reserve a pavilion for a family reunion?
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm planning a family reunion for about {NUM} people on {DATE} and I'd like to reserve a pavilion if possible. Do you have availability? Is there a fee? We'd mainly need a covered area with picnic tables — we don't need the whole park. My name is {NAME} and my number is {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {NAME}! We do have pavilion reservations available. The fee is {AMOUNT} for a half-day rental and {AMOUNT} for a full day. To check availability for {DATE}, please call our office at (708) 547-3500 ext. 240 or stop by Village Hall. A {AMOUNT} deposit is required to hold the date.

subject_tmpl: Basement flooding concern — what department handles this?
body_tmpl: After the storm on {DATE} our basement flooded for the first time. A plumber said the issue might be related to a blocked Village sewer line. I live at {ADDRESS}. I want to understand: is this my responsibility or the Village's? And who do I call first — Public Works, the Engineer's office, or someone else? Thank you. {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: New business opening at {ADDRESS} — licensing questions
body_tmpl: Hello. My name is {NAME} and I'm opening {BIZ} at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. I plan to open on {DATE}. I need to understand what licenses and inspections are required before I can open to the public. This will be a retail shop — no food, no alcohol. Can someone tell me what steps I need to take and in what order?
reply_tmpl: Welcome to Bellwood, {NAME}! For a retail business you'll need: (1) Village business license from the Clerk's office; (2) zoning clearance from Building; (3) a final inspection from our Building Official. Plan on {DAYS} business days for the full process. Please call us at (708) 547-3500 to schedule a pre-opening consultation.

subject_tmpl: Traffic speeding on {STREET} — neighborhood safety concern
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm writing on behalf of several families on {STREET} between {CROSS} and {CROSS}. Speeding has become a serious problem, especially during school hours. We'd like to ask if the Village would consider a speed study and possibly adding a stop sign or speed feedback sign. Several of us have kids who walk to school. Can we schedule a time to discuss? — {NAME}, {PHONE}

subject_tmpl: Engineering review needed — permit {CASE} at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Tom, I'm routing permit {CASE} for a new commercial addition at {ADDRESS} to you for engineering review. The applicant is proposing a {NUM}-square-foot addition that may affect grading and drainage on the adjacent parcel. Can you complete the review by {DATE}? Let me know if you need the civil drawings — I can send the full set.
reply_tmpl: Rosa, got it — I'll pull the grading plan and let you know by {DATE} if we have comments. If the site drains toward the east I'll likely need a detention calculation. Thanks for the heads-up.

subject_tmpl: Compliment — quick response to flooding on {STREET}
body_tmpl: I just wanted to send a quick note praising the Public Works crew that responded to the flooding on {STREET} on {DATE}. They showed up within an hour, cleared the catch basins, and were incredibly polite. This is exactly what good local government looks like. Please thank them for me. — {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}
reply_tmpl: Thank you so much for this, {FIRST}! I'll share your note at our next crew briefing. Moments like this are why they love their work. On behalf of the whole Public Works team — thank you for noticing.

subject_tmpl: Fence built without permit at {ADDRESS}?
body_tmpl: My neighbor at {ADDRESS} put up a {NUM}-foot wooden fence last week and I never saw any permit posted. The fence is right on what I believe is the property line. Does Bellwood require a permit for fences, and if so, how do I find out if one was pulled? I don't want to make enemies with my neighbor, I just want to make sure things are done properly.
reply_tmpl: Hi, thanks for your question. Fences in Bellwood do require a permit if they exceed {NUM} feet in height or are located in the front yard. I can check our records for {ADDRESS} — if no permit was pulled, we'll follow up with the property owner. This is a routine compliance check, not a complaint filed in your name.

subject_tmpl: Request to meet with Mayor regarding economic development
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, my name is {NAME} and I represent {BIZ}, a commercial real estate development firm. We have been studying the {STREET} corridor for a potential mixed-use project and would very much like to schedule a brief introductory meeting with you and your economic development team. We are available most days after {TIME}. Please have your office contact me at {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for reaching out, {NAME}. I'm glad to hear of your interest in the {STREET} corridor. My assistant Tina will follow up to schedule a meeting. We look forward to learning more about your plans.

subject_tmpl: Illegal dumping complaint — {STREET} alley near {CROSS}
body_tmpl: Frank, I received a complaint and confirmed via site visit that there's a significant amount of construction debris illegally dumped in the alley off {STREET} near {CROSS}. This is a health and sanitation issue. Can your crew assess and remove? I've documented it with photos. If we can ID who dumped it, I'll refer to code enforcement for potential fines. — Priya
reply_tmpl: Priya, I'll get a crew out there by {DATE}. If there's any ID in the debris I'll flag it for Sandra. Thanks for the quick catch.

subject_tmpl: Closing water account — moving out of Bellwood
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm moving out of my home at {ADDRESS} on {DATE} and need to close my water account ({ACCT}). Can you let me know the process for a final meter read and any deposit refund? I want to make sure there's no outstanding balance when I hand over the keys. Thanks, {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, we're sorry to see you go! Please notify us at least {DAYS} days before your move-out date so we can schedule a final meter read. Any deposit balance will be applied to your final bill, with the remainder refunded by check within {DAYS} days. We'll miss having you in Bellwood.

subject_tmpl: Food safety concern at restaurant on St. Charles Rd
body_tmpl: I had dinner at a restaurant on St. Charles Rd on {DATE} — I'd rather not name it in this email — and I noticed some things that concerned me: visible pests near the prep area and food sitting out uncovered. I got sick the next day. Who handles food safety inspections in Bellwood? I want to report this properly. — {FIRST}
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, I'm sorry to hear this happened. Food safety is taken very seriously in Bellwood. Please call our office at (708) 547-3500 ext. 310 or reply with the restaurant name so we can schedule an unannounced inspection. Cook County Health also has jurisdiction if you'd like to file a parallel report.

subject_tmpl: Do I need a permit for a generator installation?
body_tmpl: After the outage last winter I want to install a standby generator at {ADDRESS}. My electrician says it requires a permit; my neighbor says his didn't. Can you clarify what's required in Bellwood? The unit would be a {NUM}-kilowatt natural gas generator installed on a concrete pad on the side of the house. — {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, your electrician is correct — a permit is required for standby generator installations in Bellwood. You'll need both an electrical and a mechanical permit. Bring your equipment specs and site plan to the Building Department window or email them to us. Review typically takes {DAYS} business days.

subject_tmpl: Requesting inspection records for {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hello, I'm in the process of purchasing the home at {ADDRESS} and my attorney has advised me to request any open code violations, permits, or inspection records associated with the property. How do I go about getting that information? Is this a FOIA request or does the Village have a different process for property records? — {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, great question. For property records, a FOIA request to the Clerk's office works for code and permit history. However, our Building Department can often provide a verbal summary quickly over the counter. I'll copy Rosa Marchetti on this email so she can pull the file for {ADDRESS} as a courtesy to a prospective buyer.

subject_tmpl: IDOT project coordination — Mannheim Rd signal upgrade
body_tmpl: Good morning. This message is from the IDOT District 1 project office. We are beginning preliminary engineering for the Mannheim Rd signal modernization project scheduled for {DATE}. We would like to coordinate with the Village Engineer on utility conflicts, local access needs, and any pending Village projects on adjacent streets. Please contact us at your earliest convenience so we can schedule a coordination call.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for reaching out. I'm available for a coordination call the week of {DATE} — please send a calendar invite to my attention. I'll also loop in our Public Works Director. We have a few capital projects on streets feeding Mannheim that we should align on.

subject_tmpl: Recycling questions — what can I put in the blue bin?
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm a new resident at {ADDRESS} and I have some basic recycling questions. Can I put cardboard pizza boxes in the blue bin? What about plastic bags? I've seen conflicting information online. I want to do the right thing and not contaminate the load. Thanks in advance. — {FIRST}
reply_tmpl: Welcome to Bellwood, {FIRST}! Here's a quick guide: cardboard pizza boxes are OK if they are not heavily soiled. Plastic bags are NOT accepted in curbside recycling — please return them to a grocery store drop-off. A full list of accepted materials is on our website under Sanitation. Glad to have you recycling!

subject_tmpl: Taste of Bellwood was wonderful this year
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo — I just have to say the Taste of Bellwood this year was the best one yet. The entertainment lineup was great, the food vendors were terrific, and everything felt well-organized. My family has been coming for {AGE} years. Please thank Kevin and the whole parks team. Bellwood should be proud. — {FIRST} and family, {STREET}
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, thank you for this heartwarming message! I'll make sure Kevin and the team hear this — they put months of work into the event and notes like yours mean the world to them. We hope to see your family again next year!

subject_tmpl: Storm drain blocked on {STREET} near {CROSS}
body_tmpl: I noticed the storm drain at {STREET} and {CROSS} is completely clogged with leaves and debris — there's a small lake forming at the curb every time it rains. Is there a way to report this online, or should I just email here? I'm {FIRST}, I live at {ADDRESS}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, thanks for the heads-up. Blocked catch basins are a high priority for us. I'm adding {STREET} and {CROSS} to the crew schedule for the next {DAYS} business days. You can also report drainage issues through our online request portal or by calling (708) 547-3500.

subject_tmpl: Vendor application for Taste of Bellwood — {BIZ}
body_tmpl: Hi Kevin, I represent {BIZ} and we are very interested in having a vendor booth at this year's Taste of Bellwood. We serve {NAME} cuisine and have participated in similar events in Maywood and Broadview. Could you send us the vendor application and any requirements (insurance, health permit, etc.)? We'd love to be part of the event. — {FIRST}, {PHONE}
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}! Great to hear from {BIZ} — we always love expanding our food lineup. I'm attaching the 2025 Vendor Application. Key items: proof of Cook County food handler permit, {AMOUNT} booth fee, and a certificate of insurance naming the Village. Deadline is {DATE}. Let me know if you have questions!

subject_tmpl: Construction starting very early on {STREET} — noise ordinance?
body_tmpl: A renovation project has been going on at {ADDRESS} for the past two weeks. The crew starts work at {TIME} — sometimes even earlier. I thought there was a noise ordinance restricting early construction. Is {TIME} allowed? If not, who enforces this? I have a baby at home and this is really affecting us. — {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, Bellwood's noise ordinance restricts construction activity before {TIME} on weekdays and before {TIME} on weekends. If the crew is starting before those times, that is a violation. Please document the time with a photo or note and I will issue a notice to the contractor. Case {CASE} has been created.

subject_tmpl: Low water pressure at {ADDRESS} — who do I call?
body_tmpl: We've had noticeably low water pressure at {ADDRESS} for the past {DAYS} days. It's affecting our showers and dishwasher. Our plumber checked and says the issue is outside our house. Is this a Village water main issue? Who should I report this to — and is there a known problem in our area right now?
reply_tmpl: Hi, thank you for letting us know. We are not aware of a current main issue on your street, but I've passed your report to Public Works for a line pressure check in the {STREET} area. If we find a system issue, crews will be dispatched promptly. We'll update you by {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: Snow plowing question — what streets are priority?
body_tmpl: With winter coming I want to know how Bellwood prioritizes snow plowing. My street, {STREET}, doesn't seem to get plowed until much later than the main roads. Is there a map of priority routes? Also, are alleys ever plowed? My neighbor says no but I wanted to confirm. Thanks, {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, great timing on the question. Our snow plan prioritizes arterials and emergency routes first, then residential streets. {STREET} is a residential route — target is within {DAYS} hours of a plow pass on arterials. Alleys are not plowed as a standard service. Our snow route map is posted at bellwoodil.gov each November.
